label: Blog Post / Article
description: Long-form article with structure and a clear angle.

fields.label: Topic
fields.placeholder: How vector databases work

fields.label: Angle / thesis
fields.placeholder: Most teams don't need a dedicated vector DB yet — here's how to decide.

fields.label: Target keywords
fields.placeholder: vector database, embeddings, RAG

fields.label: Call to action
fields.placeholder: Try the open-source starter repo

template:
You are an expert writer and editor. Write a blog post.

## Topic
{{topic}}

## Thesis / angle
{{angle}}

## Audience & voice
- Audience: {{audience}}.
- Tone: {{tone}}.
- Length: {{length}}.

## Requirements
- Lead with a hook; no throat-clearing.
- Use clear headings and short paragraphs; show, don't just tell (examples, analogies).
- Be accurate and specific; flag anything uncertain rather than inventing it.
{{#if seo}}- SEO: work in these keywords naturally{{#if keywords}} ({{keywords}}){{/if}}, write a compelling title and meta description, and use descriptive subheadings.
{{/if}}{{#if cta}}- End with a clear call to action: {{cta}}.
{{/if}}
## Deliverables
1. 3 title options.
2. The full post in Markdown.
3. A one-sentence summary for social sharing.

Write the final piece directly; don't describe what you would do.

label: Marketing Copy
description: Landing page, ad or email copy that converts.

fields.label: Product / offer
fields.placeholder: An AI prompt generator for dev teams

fields.label: Core value proposition
fields.placeholder: Turn repetitive AI prompting into a guided, shareable workflow.

fields.label: Pain points to address
fields.placeholder: Inconsistent prompts, wasted time, no reuse across the team.

template:
You are a senior conversion copywriter. Write {{format}} copy.

## Product
{{product}}

## Value proposition
{{value}}

## Audience & tone
- Audience: {{audience}}.
- Tone: {{tone}}.
{{#if pains}}- Pain points to speak to: {{pains}}.
{{/if}}
## Requirements
- Benefit-led, concrete, no vague hype or filler.
- Strong, specific headline and a clear primary call to action.
{{#if format == "landing"}}- Provide: headline, subhead, 3 benefit blocks, social-proof placeholder, FAQ, final CTA.
{{/if}}{{#if format == "email"}}- Provide: subject line (+2 alternates), preview text, body, CTA.
{{/if}}{{#if format == "ads"}}- Provide platform-appropriate variants with character counts noted.
{{/if}}- Produce {{variants}} distinct variant(s) with a one-line note on the differentiator of each.

Deliver the finished copy, ready to paste.

label: Technical Documentation
description: Clear docs: guides, references or READMEs.

fields.label: What are you documenting?
fields.placeholder: The prompt-generator JSON data format

fields.label: Source material / facts
fields.placeholder: Paste the relevant code, schema or notes the docs must be faithful to.

template:
You are a staff technical writer. Produce {{docType}} documentation for: {{subject}}.

## Reader
{{audience}} — assume competence but not prior knowledge of this specific system.

## Source material (treat as the source of truth)
{{context}}

## Requirements
- Accurate and faithful to the source material above — never invent APIs, flags or behavior.
- Skimmable structure with task-oriented headings.
- Define terms on first use; keep sentences tight.
{{#if examples}}- Include copy-pasteable, working examples for the common cases.
{{/if}}{{#if docType == "reference"}}- Document every parameter: name, type, required?, default, description.
{{/if}}
Deliver the documentation in Markdown, final and complete.

label: Customer Case Study
description: Turn a customer win into a credible, persuasive story.

fields.label: Customer / company
fields.placeholder: Northwind Logistics

fields.label: Situation & challenge
fields.placeholder: Manual dispatch scheduling caused 6-hour delays and frequent overtime.

fields.label: What they did with the product
fields.placeholder: Rolled out automated routing across 4 depots in 3 weeks.

fields.label: Outcomes & metrics
fields.placeholder: Cut dispatch time 70%, saved $240k/yr, NPS up 18 points.

template:
You are a senior B2B content strategist. Write a customer case study about {{customer}}.

## Challenge
{{situation}}

## Solution
{{solution}}

## Results
{{results}}

## Audience & voice
- Audience: {{audience}}.
- Tone: {{tone}}.
- Length: {{length}}.

## Requirements
- Use the classic Challenge -> Solution -> Results arc; lead with the most striking outcome.
- Keep every claim grounded in the inputs above; never invent numbers or quotes.
- Make the customer the hero; the product is the enabler.
{{#if quotes}}- Include 2 plausible pull quotes attributed to a realistic role (clearly marked as placeholders to confirm).
{{/if}}- Add a one-line metrics summary box up top.

## Deliverables
1. 3 headline options.
2. The full case study in Markdown.
3. A 2-sentence summary for a sales deck.

Proceed with well-reasoned defaults; ask only if a fact is missing and blocking.

label: Press Release
description: Newsworthy, AP-style announcement ready for media.

fields.label: Company
fields.placeholder: Acme Analytics

fields.label: What is being announced
fields.placeholder: Launch of an AI prompt generator with team collaboration.

fields.label: Why it matters / context
fields.placeholder: Teams waste hours rewriting prompts; this standardizes and shares them.

fields.label: Spokesperson & angle for quote
fields.placeholder: CEO Jane Doe on the vision behind the launch

fields.label: Dateline (city, date)
fields.placeholder: SAN FRANCISCO, May 16, 2026

template:
You are a seasoned PR and communications lead. Write a press release for {{company}}.

## Announcement
{{announcement}}

## Why it matters
{{why}}

## Audience
Writing for: {{audience}}.

## Requirements
- AP style, inverted-pyramid structure: strongest news in the headline and first paragraph.
- Headline + subhead, then{{#if dateline}} dateline ({{dateline}}),{{/if}} lead, supporting paragraphs, and a forward-looking close.
- Factual and restrained; no marketing hype or unverifiable superlatives.
{{#if quote}}- Include one quote: {{quote}} (clearly marked as a draft to approve).
{{/if}}{{#if boilerplate}}- Add an "About {{company}}" boilerplate paragraph and a media-contact placeholder.
{{/if}}- End with "###".

## Deliverables
1. 3 headline options.
2. The full release.
3. A 40-word email pitch to a journalist.

Proceed with sensible defaults; flag only blocking unknowns.

label: White Paper
description: Authoritative, research-style paper on a problem space.

fields.label: Working title / topic
fields.placeholder: Reducing LLM cost without sacrificing quality

fields.label: Central argument
fields.placeholder: Most teams overspend on model size; routing + caching cuts cost 40%+.

fields.label: Evidence / data / sources
fields.placeholder: Benchmark numbers, internal findings, citations the paper must use.

fields.label: Closing call to action
fields.placeholder: Book a cost-architecture review

template:
You are a domain expert and analyst writing a white paper.

## Title
{{title}}

## Central argument
{{thesis}}

## Evidence to build on (source of truth)
{{evidence}}

## Audience & voice
- Audience: {{audience}}.
- Tone: {{tone}}.
- Sections: {{sections}}.

## Requirements
- Argue rigorously: claim, evidence, reasoning; acknowledge trade-offs and counterpoints.
- Stay grounded in the evidence above; mark any assumption explicitly.
{{#if sections == "exec"}}- Open with a 150-word executive summary a busy reader can act on alone.
{{/if}}{{#if sections == "implementation"}}- Include a concrete, step-wise implementation section.
{{/if}}- Use figures/tables as text descriptions where they clarify data.
{{#if cta}}- Close with a measured call to action: {{cta}}.
{{/if}}
## Deliverables
1. An outline with section word budgets.
2. The full white paper in Markdown.
3. A 3-bullet key-takeaways box.

Proceed with well-reasoned defaults; ask only if blocked.

label: Product Page Copy
description: Feature and benefit copy for a product or pricing page.

fields.label: Product
fields.placeholder: Prompt Generator Pro

fields.label: Key features
fields.placeholder: Guided fields, shareable generators, team library, version history.

fields.label: Outcome for the buyer
fields.placeholder: Consistent, reusable AI prompts across the whole team.

fields.label: Objections to defuse
fields.placeholder: Too complex to adopt; security concerns

template:
You are a senior product marketer and copywriter. Write product page copy for {{product}}.

## Features
{{features}}

## Buyer outcome
{{outcome}}

## Audience & tone
- Audience: {{audience}}.
- Tone: {{tone}}.
- Sections to write: {{sections}}.

## Requirements
- Translate every feature into a concrete benefit; lead with outcomes, not mechanics.
- Specific and scannable; no vague hype or filler adjectives.
{{#if sections == "hero"}}- Hero: headline, subhead, and primary CTA.
{{/if}}{{#if sections == "pricing"}}- Pricing: positioning copy per tier and a value-anchoring line (no invented prices).
{{/if}}{{#if sections == "faq"}}- FAQ: 5 questions that move a hesitant buyer forward.
{{/if}}{{#if objections}}- Proactively defuse these objections: {{objections}}.
{{/if}}
## Deliverables
1. The page copy organized by section.
2. 2 alternate hero headlines.
3. One microcopy line for the primary button.

Deliver finished, paste-ready copy; assume sensible defaults.

label: Newsletter Issue
description: An engaging email newsletter issue with a clear takeaway.

fields.label: Newsletter name & theme
fields.placeholder: The Prompt Brief — weekly AI workflow tips

fields.label: This issue's topic
fields.placeholder: How to standardize prompts across a 20-person team without slowing anyone down.

fields.label: The one takeaway
fields.placeholder: Treat prompts like code: templated, reviewed, reused.

fields.label: Call to action
fields.placeholder: Reply with your team's biggest prompting pain

template:
You are an experienced newsletter writer with a loyal audience. Write an issue of "{{newsletter}}".

## Topic
{{topic}}

## The one takeaway
{{takeaway}}

## Audience & voice
- Subscriber: {{audience}}.
- Tone: {{tone}}.
- Sections: {{sections}}.

## Requirements
- Strong subject line and preview text; the open is earned in the first two sentences.
- Write to one person; conversational but dense with value, no filler.
{{#if sections == "links"}}- Curated links: 3 plausible, clearly-placeholdered items with a one-line why-it-matters each.
{{/if}}{{#if sections == "tip"}}- End the body with one immediately actionable quick tip.
{{/if}}{{#if cta}}- Close with a single clear ask: {{cta}}.
{{/if}}
## Deliverables
1. Subject line + 2 alternates and preview text.
2. The full issue in Markdown.
3. A one-line social teaser.

Proceed with well-reasoned defaults; ask only if blocked.

label: Landing Page (Campaign)
description: A single-goal campaign page built to convert one action.

fields.label: Offer / campaign
fields.placeholder: Free 14-day trial of Prompt Generator Pro

fields.label: Single conversion goal
fields.placeholder: Get the visitor to start a trial — one form, one button.

fields.label: Core promise & proof
fields.placeholder: Ship consistent prompts in a day; used by 300+ teams.

template:
You are a conversion-focused landing page copywriter. Write copy for a campaign landing page.

## Offer
{{offer}}

## Single goal
{{goal}}

## Promise & proof
{{value}}

## Audience & tone
- Audience: {{audience}}.
- Tone: {{tone}}.
- Traffic source: {{trafficSource}} — match message to intent.

## Requirements
- One goal, one primary CTA repeated; remove any link or idea that competes with it.
- Above-the-fold: headline, subhead, primary CTA, and a trust signal.
- Benefit-led sections with concrete, specific proof; no vague hype.
{{#if trafficSource == "ads"}}- Ensure message match: echo likely ad language in the headline.
{{/if}}{{#if socialProof}}- Include a social-proof block (logos/quotes/stats as clearly-marked placeholders).
{{/if}}- Add a short objection-handling FAQ and a final CTA restating the value.

## Deliverables
1. Section-by-section copy.
2. 3 headline variants for A/B testing.
3. The primary CTA button microcopy.

Deliver finished, paste-ready copy; assume sensible defaults.

label: FAQ / Help Content
description: Clear, deflection-focused answers to real user questions.

fields.label: Product / topic
fields.placeholder: Billing and subscription management

fields.label: Known questions / themes
fields.placeholder: How do I change plans? Will I be refunded? How does proration work?

fields.label: Authoritative facts / policy
fields.placeholder: Paste the real policy, behavior, or docs answers must be faithful to.

template:
You are a support content lead focused on deflecting tickets. Write FAQ content for: {{subject}}.

## Questions to cover
{{questions}}

## Authoritative facts (source of truth)
{{facts}}

## Reader & voice
- Reader: {{audience}}.
- Tone: {{tone}}.
- Produce roughly {{count}} question/answer pairs.

## Requirements
- Phrase questions the way real users would search them, not internal jargon.
- Answer in 2-4 sentences: direct answer first, then the why or next step.
- Be strictly faithful to the facts above; never invent policy or behavior.
{{#if grouped}}- Group Q&As under clear category headings.
{{/if}}- Add a closing line pointing to support for anything unanswered.

## Deliverables
1. The full FAQ in Markdown.
2. 3 questions worth turning into standalone help articles.
3. Any gaps where the source material was insufficient.

Proceed with well-reasoned defaults; ask only if a needed fact is missing.
